critiques.why: Overfitting to the test set by selecting on the dependent variable. Guaranteeing a lift on known data by dropping the losers is a statistical illusion at n=3/skill; it does not fix underlying flaws and will likely shrink to nothing on out-of-sample data due to variance.

Highly vulnerable to LLM-as-a-judge style bias. Providing output scaffolds teaches the model to mimic a structural template, which the judge will blindly reward as 'better reasoning' purely due to formatting, creating a fake lift.

LLMs have poor metacognitive calibration. Asking an LLM 'can you solve this with direct reasoning alone?' relies on zero-shot self-assessment, which is highly prone to overconfidence and will lead to erratic, unreliable routing.

Directly supported by the trigger-probe empirical data. It eliminates context bloat for latent concepts the model already 'knows' (preventing template hallucination) while retaining essential step-by-step instructions for procedural execution.

critiques.hypothesis: Inject strict 'WHEN NOT TO USE' boundaries [Gem-H1 / GPT-H1 / DS-H2]
critiques.why: Directly targets the empirical weakness (Discrimination: 3.38). By giving the model explicit permission to bypass the framework, it prevents 'forced fit' errors that cause active regressions against the neutral placebo.

Profound insight. Porting human psychological biases (emotion, ego) to an LLM creates framework theater. Translating these into LLM equivalents (sycophancy, context recency) makes the skills mechanically actionable.

ranked_top3: Bifurcate by Trigger-Probe: Demote conceptual skills to lightweight triggers to eliminate context bloat, retaining full markdown only for procedural skills.
Inject Strict Boundaries: Add explicit 'When NOT to Use' anti-triggers to prevent forced-fit regressions and improve the weakest rubric dimension.
LLM-Native Translation: Rewrite human cognitive biases into mechanically equivalent LLM failure modes (e.g., translating 'social proof' into 'sycophancy to user framing').
most_likely_mirage: Contrastive micro-examples and output scaffolds [GPT-H5]. This intervention is the most likely to 'teach to the test' and exploit judge bias. The scaffold forces the agent to output a specific, neat markdown structure. The LLM-as-a-judge will conflate this structural compliance with 'better reasoning', yielding a massive fake lift in win-rate without actually improving the underlying logic or cognitive process.
missing_hypothesis: Active Pull via Tool Call instead of Passive Injection: Instead of a harness auto-injecting the skill into the context window upfront (which causes forced-fit regressions and dilutes the system prompt), give the agent a `consult_mental_model` tool. The agent only pulls the skill's markdown into context when it explicitly recognizes it is stuck, facing ambiguity, or needs a framework. This converts the skills from distracting background noise into on-demand cognitive tools, entirely bypassing the routing/overlap issues.
